# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Data Processing Pipeline Script
# Uses temporary files in /tmp/ with predictable names for tracking

# Define temp file paths using PID for tracking
TEMP_INPUT="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"
TEMP_PROCESSED="/tmp/myapp-$$-processed.tmp"
TEMP_RESULTS="/tmp/myapp-$$-results.tmp"
TEMP_SUMMARY="/tmp/myapp-$$-summary.tmp"

echo "=== Data Processing Pipeline ==="
echo "PID: $$"
echo "Temp files will use prefix: /tmp/myapp-$$"
echo ""

# Cleanup function to remove temp files on exit
cleanup() {
    echo ""
    echo "=== Cleanup ==="
    echo "Removing temporary files..."
    rm -f "$TEMP_INPUT" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" "$TEMP_RESULTS" "$TEMP_SUMMARY"
    echo "Cleanup complete."
}

# Register cleanup on EXIT, INT, TERM signals
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# STAGE 1: Generate / Ingest raw data into temp file
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "--- Stage 1: Data Ingestion ---"
echo "Writing raw data to $TEMP_INPUT"

cat > "$TEMP_INPUT" <<EOF
2024-01-15,server-alpha,cpu,78.5
2024-01-15,server-beta,cpu,92.3
2024-01-15,server-gamma,cpu,45.1
2024-01-15,server-alpha,memory,65.2
2024-01-15,server-beta,memory,88.7
2024-01-15,server-gamma,memory,32.4
2024-01-15,server-alpha,disk,55.0
2024-01-15,server-beta,disk,71.8
2024-01-15,server-gamma,disk,20.5
2024-01-16,server-alpha,cpu,81.2
2024-01-16,server-beta,cpu,95.6
2024-01-16,server-gamma,cpu,50.3
2024-01-16,server-alpha,memory,70.1
2024-01-16,server-beta,memory,91.2
2024-01-16,server-gamma,memory,35.8
2024-01-16,server-alpha,disk,58.3
2024-01-16,server-beta,disk,75.4
2024-01-16,server-gamma,disk,22.1
EOF

RECORD_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$TEMP_INPUT")
echo "Ingested $RECORD_COUNT records into $TEMP_INPUT"

# Verify the temp file exists and has content
if [[ ! -s "$TEMP_INPUT" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: Input temp file is empty or missing!" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo ""

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# STAGE 2: Process / Transform data - filter and enrich
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "--- Stage 2: Data Processing ---"
echo "Reading from $TEMP_INPUT, writing processed data to $TEMP_PROCESSED"

# Add a status column based on threshold values
while IFS=',' read -r date server metric value; do
    # Determine status based on metric type and value
    status="OK"
    int_value=${value%.*}  # truncate to integer for comparison

    case "$metric" in
        cpu)
            if (( int_value >= 90 )); then
                status="CRITICAL"
            elif (( int_value >= 75 )); then
                status="WARNING"
            fi
            ;;
        memory)
            if (( int_value >= 85 )); then
                status="CRITICAL"
            elif (( int_value >= 60 )); then
                status="WARNING"
            fi
            ;;
        disk)
            if (( int_value >= 80 )); then
                status="CRITICAL"
            elif (( int_value >= 70 )); then
                status="WARNING"
            fi
            ;;
    esac

    echo "$date,$server,$metric,$value,$status"
done < "$TEMP_INPUT" > "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

PROCESSED_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$TEMP_PROCESSED")
echo "Processed $PROCESSED_COUNT records into $TEMP_PROCESSED"

# Re-read the processed file to show a preview
echo ""
echo "Preview of processed data (first 5 records):"
head -5 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | column -t -s','

echo ""

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# STAGE 3: Analyze data - generate results
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "--- Stage 3: Data Analysis ---"
echo "Reading from $TEMP_PROCESSED, writing results to $TEMP_RESULTS"

# Count alerts by severity
critical_count=$(grep -c "CRITICAL" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" || true)
warning_count=$(grep -c "WARNING" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" || true)
ok_count=$(grep -c ",OK$" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" || true)

# Write analysis results to results temp file
{
    echo "=== ANALYSIS RESULTS ==="
    echo "Report Generated: $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')"
    echo "Pipeline PID: $$"
    echo ""
    echo "--- Alert Summary ---"
    echo "CRITICAL: $critical_count"
    echo "WARNING:  $warning_count"
    echo "OK:       $ok_count"
    echo "TOTAL:    $PROCESSED_COUNT"
    echo ""
    echo "--- Critical Alerts Detail ---"
} > "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Extract and append critical alerts from the processed temp file
grep "CRITICAL" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | while IFS=',' read -r date server metric value status; do
    echo "  [$date] $server - $metric at ${value}%"
done >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Append warning alerts
{
    echo ""
    echo "--- Warning Alerts Detail ---"
} >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"

grep "WARNING" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | while IFS=',' read -r date server metric value status; do
    echo "  [$date] $server - $metric at ${value}%"
done >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"

echo "Analysis complete. Results written to $TEMP_RESULTS"
echo ""

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# STAGE 4: Aggregate per-server summary
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "--- Stage 4: Server Summary ---"
echo "Reading from $TEMP_PROCESSED, writing summary to $TEMP_SUMMARY"

{
    echo "=== PER-SERVER SUMMARY ==="
    echo ""

    # Get unique servers from the processed temp file
    servers=$(cut -d',' -f2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | sort -u)

    for server in $servers; do
        echo "Server: $server"

        # Extract metrics for this server from the processed file
        server_criticals=$(grep "$server" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | grep -c "CRITICAL" || true)
        server_warnings=$(grep "$server" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | grep -c "WARNING" || true)

        # Calculate average values per metric from processed temp file
        for metric in cpu memory disk; do
            values=$(grep "$server.*$metric" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | cut -d',' -f4)
            if [[ -n "$values" ]]; then
                avg=$(echo "$values" | awk '{sum+=$1; count++} END {printf "%.1f", sum/count}')
                echo "  Avg $metric: ${avg}%"
            fi
        done

        echo "  Critical alerts: $server_criticals"
        echo "  Warning alerts:  $server_warnings"

        # Determine overall server health
        if (( server_criticals > 0 )); then
            echo "  Overall Status:  *** NEEDS ATTENTION ***"
        elif (( server_warnings > 0 )); then
            echo "  Overall Status:  MONITOR"
        else
            echo "  Overall Status:  HEALTHY"
        fi
        echo ""
    done
} > "$TEMP_SUMMARY"

echo "Summary written to $TEMP_SUMMARY"
echo ""

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# STAGE 5: Final output - read from all temp files and display
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "--- Stage 5: Final Report ---"
echo ""

# Read and display the results file
cat "$TEMP_RESULTS"
echo ""

# Read and display the summary file
cat "$TEMP_SUMMARY"

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Display temp file tracking info
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "=== Pipeline Temp File Tracking ==="
echo "The following temp files were used in this pipeline (PID: $$):"
echo ""

for tmpfile in "$TEMP_INPUT" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" "$TEMP_RESULTS" "$TEMP_SUMMARY"; do
    if [[ -f "$tmpfile" ]]; then
        filesize=$(wc -c < "$tmpfile")
        linecount=$(wc -l < "$tmpfile")
        echo "  $tmpfile  (${filesize} bytes, ${linecount} lines)"
    else
        echo "  $tmpfile  [MISSING]"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "=== Pipeline Complete ==="
echo "Temp files will be cleaned up on exit."

# Cleanup happens automatically via the trap
exit 0
